title: "volume create" fails if a volume already exists, unlike Docker
body:
### Issue Description

Switching from Docker to Podman, a program - which wanted to create a volume if it did not already exist, to ensure it existed - started failing.

In short, the command `docker volume create foo` started failing if the volume already exists, when `docker` is an alias for `podman`.

The cause seems to be that Docker allows the volume to already exist by default, per https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/volume/create/:

> If you specify a volume name already in use on the current driver, Docker assumes you want to reuse the existing volume and doesn't return an error.

However, podman is the opposite, per https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-volume-create.1.html:

> --ignore
> Don’t fail if the named volume already exists, instead just print the name. Note that the new options are not applied to the existing volume.

I get that exact compatibility of flags and semantics is perhaps hard to do, but I'm not sure how to make this program work on both Docker and Podman if they have exactly opposite defaults, and only Podman has the "ignore" flag.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman volume create foo`
2. `podman volume create foo`


### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman volume create foo
foo
$ podman volume create foo
Error: volume with name foo already exists: volume already exists
```

### Describe the results you expected

Both commands to succeed, like Docker.

### podman info output

comments.body:
I rather not chnage the podman behavior now. Given it took that long for this be noticed I doubt it is a common thing and chaining the podman behavior could be a breaking change.

I think long term for docker compat it is best to use the docker compat API we expose, i.e. use the docker cli against the podman socket. In the docker API level we can make required fixes any time without affecting direct podman users.

I guess I don't oppose that strategy, but currently with Fedora 42 the way that podman is set up as a docker drop-in replacement is basically an alias. So if the long-term strategy should be to replace the socket/daemon but not the CLI tool, then I assume Fedora should switch to that at some point.
Of course, the easier solution would be to teach Docker about the `--ignore` flag, but I'm not sure if they would be convinced :) I generally agree with your default behavior, for what it's worth.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this bot is pointless.

It is not pointless, the bot is useful to maintainers.

For reference https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/16068 seems to track it for docker but I doubt they ever change this given how long this has been open.

I don't know if there is anything we can do, I don't want to change the podman behavior so I guess closing as wontfix would be logical.
cc @mheon

I wonder if we shouldn't consider a "Strict Docker Compatibility" option in containers.conf that would change behaviors like this. We'd default to "false" to not break folks. But that's really a separate feature discussion (and would be potentially a significant amount of work - we might end up needing different output structs, for example!) and should be in a separate issue. I'm fine with closing this one as such.

I'd be fine with that. I understand that maximum compatibility with Docker can be at odds with good defaults at times. However, I work on projects where other team members use Docker, so I can't afford hard incompatibilities between the two tools like this one.

As a potential first-time contributor, it's not a good-first-issue, as it's unclear what's the desired behaviour @Honny1 .

Is it possible for podman to enable compatibility with docker when it's run as docker, i.e. `argv[0] == 'docker'` - is it something implemented/planned and is there architecture for such configuration?

edit:

Also, if and when https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/16068 is fixed,  what's the correct approach for podman - when to change the default in compat mode?

Ah, I didn't read the whole conversation. Thanks for pointing that out, @rafalkrupinski. I only read the description when I added the labels. At first glance, it looked like a simple default flip since we were on a version before Podman 6. However, it seems to be more problematic than I expected. It looks like we don't want to get this fixed after all.
Does it work correctly in our docker compat API? I think the only sane option for a "Strict Docker Compatibility" mode it use the docker client and point it at the docker socket.
